trivia_bullets.text: MKM's detective theme extends to its black-aligned cards with the 'unscrupulous' variation: investigators who bend or break procedure to get results. The Ravnican Agency of Magicological Investigations (R.A.M.I.) exists as a cross-guild forensic authority, but black-aligned operatives in the set represent the shadow side of investigation — extortion, evidence theft, coercion — methods the official agencies can't use. [magic.wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Murders at Karlov Manor]

Flavor text: "Obtaining evidence through the 'proper' channels can take weeks of paperwork. Sometimes justice just can't wait." The ironic framing puts 'proper' in scare-quotes — mirroring the Rot Farm Mortipede's 'recycling' quote from the same set, a pattern of MKM black cards using quotation-mark irony to signal institutional bad faith. [Scryfall]

Type line: Creature — Elf Detective. The 'Detective' creature type was introduced in MKM as a mechanical marker for the set's investigation theme. The Elf subtype places this figure in the broader Ravnican population — Elves appear across multiple Ravnica guilds, particularly Golgari and Simic. [Scryfall type_line]

The ETB effect forces an opponent to exile a card from hand — a 'lose a card forever' effect rather than discard (opponent doesn't discard to a graveyard; the card is gone). At 2 mana for a 1/1 with a one-sided exile effect, it is a role-player in aggressive black strategies but not a staple. EDHREC rank 20,243; penny_rank 1,322, indicating it sees very limited competitive play. [Scryfall]

related_cards.name: Alquist Proft, Master Sleuth
related_cards.note: Set protagonist; the 'proper channels' Unscrupulous Agent disdains are presumably his methods

related_cards.name: Thoughtseize
related_cards.note: Canonical black hand-disruption spell; Unscrupulous Agent's exile effect is a weaker but permanent-loss variation on the same design space

related_cards.name: Extract
related_cards.note: Earlier card with exile-from-hand effect; precedent for this design space
